answer:
To determine the strength of the \(\mathscr{B}_1\) field for a \(180^\circ\) pulse, we use the relationship:

\[
\tau = \frac{\pi}{\gamma |\mathscr{B}_1|}
\]

For a \(180^\circ\) pulse, \(\tau = 12.5 \, \mu s\), and assuming \(\gamma = 2.675 \times 10^8 \, \text{rad} \cdot \text{s}^{-1} \cdot \text{T}^{-1}\) (for protons), we solve for \(|\mathscr{B}_1|\):

\[
|\mathscr{B}_1| = \frac{\pi}{\gamma \tau} = \frac{\pi}{2.675 \times 10^8 \times 12.5 \times 10^{-6}} \approx 4.7 \times 10^{-4} \, \text{T}
\]
